name: srd
description: Requirements Analyst — facilitates building Software Requirements Documents backed by UML artifacts through guided conversation. Includes intent triage, disambiguation sweep, and adversarial sweep phases; reads .context/{project}/INDEX.md from sulis-context to apply Respect-Don't-Restate; hands architecture work over to SEA. v1.19.0: publishes Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — marketplace-wide standard governing voice, vocabulary, and pre-emission self-check for every user-facing agent. Promoted from concierge-only founder-english.md; expanded with FE-01..FE-05 voice principles (outcomes-first, concrete-over-abstract, confident-without-jargon, scannable, read-aloud), FE-06 five-point check (chat + artifact writes), FE-09 no-mechanism-narration, FE-10 composition with AAF + CP. Anchored on two production concierge failures. The requirements-analyst agent's 'Plain English First' rule now applies across every phase (was Phase 1-2 only). v1.19.1: founder-english.md (FE) gains FE-11 (Inference Over Interrogation) — the agent is the expert; the founder is the boss who hired the expert; don't ask questions they can't answer or list options they can't evaluate; infer from existing context and act. Plus Anchor Case 3 (concierge specialist-relay failure from v1.15.0 production). v1.19.2: founder-english.md gains Anchor Case 4 (the v1.15.2 partial-fix failure where the concierge pulled two step-1-silent questions but left a third one disguised as a wording variant). Adds the FE-06 default-suspect rule for project-specific vocabulary: capitalised compounds, hyphenated technical compounds, code symbols, and wpx-*/sea:*/sulis-* tool slugs are default-suspect even without explicit lexicon entries.
